skill.name: genkit
skill.description: Build production-ready AI workflows using Firebase Genkit. Use when creating flows, tool-calling agents, RAG pipelines, multi-agent systems, or deploying AI to Firebase/Cloud Run. Supports TypeScript, Go, and Python with Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, and Vertex AI plugins.
skill.summary: Build production-ready AI workflows using Firebase Genkit with type-safe flows, tool-calling agents, and RAG pipelines.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 113 static findings are false positives. External commands are legitimate bash CLI commands for npm/Genkit installation. Network references are documentation URLs. Environment variable access uses placeholders with security warnings. The skill is a genuine Firebase Genkit documentation file for building AI workflows.

low_findings.title: CLI Installation Command (curl | bash)
low_findings.description: The skill documents the official Genkit CLI installation method using `curl -sL cli.genkit.dev | bash`. This is a common installation pattern endorsed by Firebase for their Genkit tool.

content.user_title: Build AI Workflows with Firebase Genkit
content.value_statement: Building AI workflows requires understanding complex orchestration patterns, deployment configurations, and model integrations. This skill provides production-ready templates and best practices for creating type-safe flows, tool-calling agents, and RAG pipelines with Genkit.
actual_capabilities: Create type-safe AI flows with Zod input/output schemas and automatic tracing
Implement tool-calling agents with custom tools and agentic loops
Build RAG pipelines using Pinecone, pgvector, Chroma, or Firestore vector stores
Deploy flows as Firebase Cloud Functions, Express endpoints, or Cloud Run services
Use streaming responses for real-time token output in chat applications
Orchestrate multi-agent systems with specialized subflow coordination
limitations: Requires API keys for model providers (Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
Deployment targets are limited to Firebase, Cloud Run, or Express servers
Does not include pre-trained models; requires external model API access
Local development requires Node.js and the Genkit CLI installation

use_cases.title: Build Document Q&A with RAG
use_cases.description: Create a flow that retrieves relevant documents from a vector database and generates answers grounded in the retrieved context.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building AI-powered knowledge bases

use_cases.title: Create Tool-Calling Chatbot
use_cases.description: Implement an agent that automatically calls custom tools like weather lookups, web search, or database queries to answer user questions.
use_cases.target_user: Product engineers adding AI capabilities to applications

use_cases.title: Deploy Multi-Model API
use_cases.description: Wrap LLM calls into deployable HTTP endpoints that support multiple model providers with unified type-safe interfaces.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers standardizing AI integration patterns

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Genkit flow called summarizeFlow that takes text input and returns a summary with key points. Use Zod for input/output schemas.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Getting started with Genkit basics

prompt_templates.title: Add Tool Calling to a Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a getWeather tool to my existing flow that calls a weather API. The flow should automatically decide when to call the tool based on user input.

prompt_templates.title: Build RAG Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a RAG flow that indexes documents into Pinecone and retrieves relevant chunks to answer user questions. Include proper error handling for null outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy to Firebase
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my Genkit flow as a Firebase Cloud Function using onCallGenkit. Use Firebase Secrets for API key management and set up proper CORS handling.

output_examples.input: Create a flow that takes a topic and returns a structured response with title, summary, and related topics
output_examples.output: Flow created: topicFlow with Zod schemas. Input: { topic: string }, Output: { title: string, summary: string, relatedTopics: string[] }. Ready for deployment via genkit flow:run topicFlow.

output_examples.input: Add a searchWeb tool to my agent that retrieves current information
output_examples.output: Tool defined: searchWeb with z.object({ query: z.string() }) schema. Added to agent flow tools array. Agent will now automatically call searchWeb when user asks about current events.

best_practices: Define Zod schemas for all flow inputs and outputs to enable Dev UI labeled fields and runtime validation
Store API keys in environment variables; use Firebase Secrets (defineSecret) for production deployments
Use ai.run() to wrap non-Genkit code for trace visibility in the Developer UI
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in source code; always use environment variables or Firebase Secrets
Do not call generate() outside a flow if you need tracing and observability
Do not call genkit start without a command; always pass -- <your-run-command>

faq.question: What model providers does Genkit support?
faq.answer: Genkit supports Google AI (Gemini), Vertex AI, OpenAI (GPT models), Anthropic (Claude), AWS Bedrock, Ollama (local models), DeepSeek, and xAI (Grok).

faq.question: How do I deploy a Genkit flow to production?
faq.answer: Use onCallGenkit() for Firebase Cloud Functions, expressHandler() for Express servers, or the Cloud Run deployment command for containerized deployments.

faq.question: What is the Developer UI and how do I use it?
faq.answer: The Developer UI runs at localhost:4000 and provides a visual interface for testing flows, inspecting traces, testing prompts, and comparing model outputs.

faq.question: How does tool calling work in Genkit?
faq.answer: Define tools with ai.defineTool() using Zod schemas, then pass them to generate() with the tools array. Set returnToolRequests: false for auto-execution.

faq.question: Can I use Genkit with local models?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Ollama plugin (genkitx-ollama) to run local models like Llama 2, Mistral, or other Ollama-supported models without API calls.

faq.question: How do I handle streaming responses?
faq.answer: Use defineFlow with streamSchema and sendChunk in the callback. The client consumes stream data via the stream property of the returned object.
